  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.h", "symbol": "onDiscoveryResponse"},
    {"repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.h", "symbol": "genericHandleResponse"},
    {"repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.h", "symbol": "trySendDiscoveryRequests"},
    {"repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/new_grpc_mux_impl.h", "symbol": "onDiscoveryResponse"},
    {"repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/new_grpc_mux_impl.h", "symbol": "trySendDiscoveryRequests"},
    {"repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/subscription_state.h", "symbol": "handleResponse"},
    {"repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/subscription_state.h", "symbol": "getNextRequestWithAck"},
    {"repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_stream.h", "symbol": "onReceiveMessage"},
    {"repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_stream.h", "symbol": "sendMessage"},
    {"repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/pausable_ack_queue.h", "symbol": "push"},
    {"repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/pausable_ack_queue.h", "symbol": "popFront"},
    {"repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/watch_map.h", "symbol": "updateWatchInterest"},
    {"repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/watch_map.h", "symbol": "onConfigUpdate"}
  ],
  "text": "The Envoy ADS (Aggregated Discovery Service) gRPC client implementation consists of multiple layers coordinating the xDS protocol:\n\n**Core Mux Implementations** (source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/):\n- xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.h/cc: Template-based unified mux supporting both SotW (State-of-the-World) and Delta xDS modes through GrpcMuxDelta and GrpcMuxSotw specializations\n- new_grpc_mux_impl.h/cc: Unified delta-xDS-only implementation using DeltaSubscriptionState\n- grpc_mux_impl.h/cc: Legacy implementation supporting DiscoveryRequest/Response messages\n\n**Protocol State Management** (Delta and SotW xDS):\n- xds_mux/subscription_state.h: Abstract base class (BaseSubscriptionState) managing per-type_url protocol state\n- xds_mux/delta_subscription_state.h/cc: Delta xDS state machine tracking per-resource versions, resource subscriptions, and state transitions\n- xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.h/cc: State-of-the-World xDS state machine tracking version_info and requested resources\n- delta_subscription_state.h/cc: Legacy delta subscription implementation\n\n**gRPC Stream Communication** (source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/):\n- grpc_stream.h: Template-based GrpcStream<RequestProto, ResponseProto> implementing rate limiting, backoff retry logic, and stream lifecycle (establishNewStream, sendMessage, onReceiveMessage)\n- grpc_stream_interface.h: Abstract interface for stream implementations\n- grpc_mux_failover.h: Failover handler managing primary/failover xDS server connections with state transitions\n\n**Resource Watch Management**:\n- watch_map.h/cc: Multiplexes multiple watches (subscriptions) of the same resource type, tracking interest changes and delivering updates via SubscriptionCallbacks\n\n**ACK/NACK Processing**:\n- update_ack.h: Data structure for ACK/NACK messages containing nonce, type_url, and error details\n- pausable_ack_queue.h/cc: Queue managing ACK ordering with pause/resume per type_url to handle head-of-line blocking in ADS\n\n**Supporting Infrastructure**:\n- grpc_mux_context.h: Context and configuration for mux creation\n- eds_resources_cache_impl.h/cc: Caching layer for ClusterLoadAssignment (EDS) resources\n- xds_source_id.h/cc: Source identification for resource tracking\n- grpc_subscription_impl.h/cc: Subscription implementation for individual resource types\n- grpc_subscription_factory.h/cc: Factory for creating subscriptions\n- grpc_collection_subscription_factory.h/cc: Factory for collection-based subscriptions\n\nThe flow: gRPC stream receives DiscoveryResponse → GrpcStream.onReceiveMessage → GrpcMux.onDiscoveryResponse → SubscriptionState.handleResponse (which validates and updates state) → WatchMap.onConfigUpdate (delivers to interested watches) → ACK generated via SubscriptionState.getNextRequestWithAck → PausableAckQueue.push → GrpcMux.trySendDiscoveryRequests → GrpcStream.sendMessage (sends DeltaDiscoveryRequest/DiscoveryRequest with nonce ACK)."
}

conversation_events.text:
# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

Use `sg_list_repos` to discover available repositories before searching.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

# Envoy xDS Aggregated Discovery Service Server

## Your Task

Find all C++ source files in envoyproxy/envoy that implement the ADS (Aggregated Discovery Service) gRPC server: the gRPC stream handler, the resource subscription tracking, the delta-xDS state machine, and the ACK/NACK processing.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the platform domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2, sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367, sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6, sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2` (envoyproxy/envoy)
- `sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367` (envoyproxy/data-plane-api)
- `sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6` (envoyproxy/go-control-plane)
- `sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e` (grpc/grpc)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find all C++ source files implementing the ADS (Aggregated Discovery Service) gRPC server in envoyproxy/envoy. Let me start by searching the repository systematically.
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the ADS implementation. Let me create the answer file with all the relevant C++ source files.

Perfect! I've created a comprehensive analysis of all the C++ source files that implement the ADS (Aggregated Discovery Service) gRPC client system in Envoy. 

## Summary

The answer includes **31 C++ source files** organized into key components:

1. **Core Mux Implementations** (3 implementations):
   - Unified template-based mux (xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl)
   - New unified delta-only mux (new_grpc_mux_impl)
   - Legacy mux implementation (grpc_mux_impl)

2. **Delta/SotW xDS State Machines** (6 files):
   - Abstract base subscription state
   - Delta xDS state machine (both new and legacy versions)
   - State-of-the-World xDS state machine

3. **gRPC Stream Handling** (3 files):
   - GrpcStream template with rate limiting and retry logic
   - Abstract stream interface
   - Failover mechanism for primary/failover servers

4. **Resource Subscription Tracking**:
   - WatchMap for multiplexing multiple watches per resource type

5. **ACK/NACK Processing** (3 files):
   - UpdateAck structure
   - PausableAckQueue for ordering ACKs with pause support

6. **Supporting Infrastructure** (7 files):
   - Mux context, subscription factories, EDS resource caching, etc.

The narrative in the answer explains the complete message flow through these components: from receiving gRPC responses, validating state, delivering to interested watches, generating ACKs, and sending the next request with proper nonce acknowledgment.

repo:^github.com/envoyproxy/envoy$ ADS server implementation discovery service

    {"repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_stream.h", "symbol": "onReceiveMessage"},
    {"repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_stream.h", "symbol": "sendMessage"},
    {"repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/pausable_ack_queue.h", "symbol": "push"},
    {"repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/pausable_ack_queue.h", "symbol": "popFront"},
    {"repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/watch_map.h", "symbol": "updateWatchInterest"},
    {"repo": "github.com/envoyproxy/envoy", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/watch_map.h", "symbol": "onConfigUpdate"}
  ],
  "text": "The Envoy ADS (Aggregated Discovery Service) gRPC client implementation consists of multiple layers coordinating the xDS protocol:\n\n**Core Mux Implementations** (source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/):\n- xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.h/cc: Template-based unified mux supporting both SotW (State-of-the-World) and Delta xDS modes through GrpcMuxDelta and GrpcMuxSotw specializations\n- new_grpc_mux_impl.h/cc: Unified delta-xDS-only implementation using DeltaSubscriptionState\n- grpc_mux_impl.h/cc: Legacy implementation supporting DiscoveryRequest/Response messages\n\n**Protocol State Management** (Delta and SotW xDS):\n- xds_mux/subscription_state.h: Abstract base class (BaseSubscriptionState) managing per-type_url protocol state\n- xds_mux/delta_subscription_state.h/cc: Delta xDS state machine tracking per-resource versions, resource subscriptions, and state transitions\n- xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.h/cc: State-of-the-World xDS state machine tracking version_info and requested resources\n- delta_subscription_state.h/cc: Legacy delta subscription implementation\n\n**gRPC Stream Communication** (source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/):\n- grpc_stream.h: Template-based GrpcStream<RequestProto, ResponseProto> implementing rate limiting, backoff retry logic, and stream lifecycle (establishNewStream, sendMessage, onReceiveMessage)\n- grpc_stream_interface.h: Abstract interface for stream implementations\n- grpc_mux_failover.h: Failover handler managing primary/failover xDS server connections with state transitions\n\n**Resource Watch Management**:\n- watch_map.h/cc: Multiplexes multiple watches (subscriptions) of the same resource type, tracking interest changes and delivering updates via SubscriptionCallbacks\n\n**ACK/NACK Processing**:\n- update_ack.h: Data structure for ACK/NACK messages containing nonce, type_url, and error details\n- pausable_ack_queue.h/cc: Queue managing ACK ordering with pause/resume per type_url to handle head-of-line blocking in ADS\n\n**Supporting Infrastructure**:\n- grpc_mux_context.h: Context and configuration for mux creation\n- eds_resources_cache_impl.h/cc: Caching layer for ClusterLoadAssignment (EDS) resources\n- xds_source_id.h/cc: Source identification for resource tracking\n- grpc_subscription_impl.h/cc: Subscription implementation for individual resource types\n- grpc_subscription_factory.h/cc: Factory for creating subscriptions\n- grpc_collection_subscription_factory.h/cc: Factory for collection-based subscriptions\n\nThe flow: gRPC stream receives DiscoveryResponse → GrpcStream.onReceiveMessage → GrpcMux.onDiscoveryResponse → SubscriptionState.handleResponse (which validates and updates state) → WatchMap.onConfigUpdate (delivers to interested watches) → ACK generated via SubscriptionState.getNextRequestWithAck → PausableAckQueue.push → GrpcMux.trySendDiscoveryRequests → GrpcStream.sendMessage (sends DeltaDiscoveryRequest/DiscoveryRequest with nonce ACK)."
}
